component.name: Gaia Sky
component.developer_name: Zentrum für Astronomie der Universität Heidelberg

description.p: Gaia Sky is an astronomy visualization platform for desktop and VR that runs on Linux, Windows and macOS. It is developed in the framework of ESA's Gaia mission to chart about 1 billion stars of our Galaxy in the Gaia group of the Astronomisches Rechen-Institut (ZAH, Universität Heidelberg). Explore the Solar System, the Milky Way, and even the most distant galaxies of our Universe with up-to-date, real scientific data.
This release focuses on QoL improvements, and contains several important bugfixes:

ul.li: Redesigned "Load dataset" dialog with two tabs: "Load installed dataset" and "Load file".
Added support for server dataset images and mission dataset selection (sets time and camera focus).
New line shading method using screen-space derivatives for higher quality.
Added limb fade to cloud layer based on screen-space pixel counts.
Added PBR support in Wavefront .mtl files and intrinsic opacity for materials.
Extensive refactoring of settings handling (removed static references).
Fixed crash with asterisks in Windows paths (shader cache).
Fixed shader positioning bugs in model-based particle sets.
Fixed color picker crash and various dataset widget resizing issues.
Japanese translation is now fully complete.
Complete migration of Gradle build scripts to Kotlin DSL.

screenshot.caption: Rendering of Earth's surface with the aurora borealis.
Detail view of Saturn and its rings
The inner Solar System with planets and asteroids
Solar eclipse representation on the surface of Earth
The Trifid nebula rendered as a volume
The city of Rome on the surface of Earth, using a virtual texture with Sentinel-II data
